intro.title: 1. Introduction
paragraphs: {{appName}} ("we", "us", or "our") is a web-based theater ticketing service operated by {{operatorName}} (the "Operator"). This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal data when you use our Service at {{appUrl}}.
By creating an account or making a booking, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

dataCollected.title: 2. Data We Collect
paragraphs: We collect the following categories of personal data:
Account data: your name, email address, phone number, and password (stored in hashed form) when you register.
Booking data: the events, performances, and seats you book, your order history, booking references, and QR codes issued to you.
Payment data: when a paid transaction is processed, payment details are handled directly by our payment processor and are not stored on our servers beyond what is strictly necessary.
Usage data: IP address, browser type, device information, pages visited, and timestamps, collected automatically when you interact with the Service.
Communication data: messages you send us via email or through the Service.

dataUse.title: 3. How We Use Your Data
paragraphs: We use your personal data to:
Deliver the Service: process bookings, issue tickets and QR codes, and manage your account.
Communicate with you: send booking confirmations, ticket emails, and important Service updates.
Send marketing communications: only if you have given explicit consent, we may send newsletters or promotional offers.
Improve the Service: analyze usage patterns to enhance functionality and user experience.
Comply with legal obligations: retain records as required by applicable law, including Italian fiscal regulations.
Prevent fraud and abuse: protect the security and integrity of the Service.

legalBasis.title: 4. Legal Basis for Processing (GDPR)
paragraphs: We process your data on the following legal bases under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Italian Personal Data Protection Code (D.Lgs. 196/2003 as amended by D.Lgs. 101/2018):
Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR): processing your account and booking data is necessary to provide the Service you requested.
Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR): marketing communications and optional analytics cookies are processed only with your explicit consent, which you may withdraw at any time.
Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR): we retain certain records to comply with Italian fiscal and accounting laws.
Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR): fraud prevention, security monitoring, and basic analytics to improve the Service.

dataSharing.title: 5. Data Sharing and Third Parties
paragraphs: We do not sell your personal data. We may share it only in the following circumstances:
Service providers: we engage trusted processors (e.g., hosting providers, email delivery services, payment processors) who act on our instructions and are bound by data processing agreements.
Theater operators: booking details are shared with the theater or event organizer for admission and capacity management purposes.
Legal requirements: we may disclose data to competent authorities when required by law or to protect our legal rights.
All transfers outside the European Economic Area are subject to appropriate safeguards (e.g., Standard Contractual Clauses).

dataRetention.title: 6. Data Retention
paragraphs: We retain your data only as long as necessary for the purposes described in this Policy:
Account data is retained for as long as your account remains active. Upon account deletion, we erase your personal data within 30 days, subject to legal retention requirements.
Booking and payment records are retained for 10 years in accordance with Italian fiscal law (art. 2220 c.c.).
Usage logs are anonymized or deleted within 12 months.
Marketing consent records are retained until you withdraw consent, plus 2 years for compliance evidence.

yourRights.title: 7. Your Rights
paragraphs: Under GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
Right of access (Art. 15): obtain a copy of the data we hold about you.
Right to rectification (Art. 16): request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.
Right to erasure (Art. 17): request deletion of your data where there is no overriding legal basis for retention.
Right to restriction (Art. 18): request that we limit processing of your data in certain circumstances.
Right to data portability (Art. 20): receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Right to object (Art. 21): object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing purposes.
Right to withdraw consent: where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw it at any time without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at the address below. We will respond within 30 days. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority (Garante per la protezione dei dati personali — www.garanteprivacy.it).

cookies.title: 8. Cookies and Similar Technologies
paragraphs: We use cookies and similar technologies as follows:
Strictly necessary cookies: required for the Service to function (e.g., authentication session, language preference, consent record). These cannot be disabled.
Analytics cookies: used to understand how users interact with the Service. These are only set with your consent and can be disabled in your cookie preferences.
We do not use advertising or tracking cookies for third-party marketing purposes.
You can manage your cookie preferences at any time via the cookie settings banner or your browser settings.

childrens.title: 9. Children's Privacy
paragraphs: The Service is not directed at children under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 16. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data without parental consent, please contact us and we will promptly delete it.

changes.title: 10. Changes to This Policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make material changes, we will notify you by email or via a prominent notice on the Service, and update the "last updated" date at the top of this page.
Your continued use of the Service after changes become effective constitutes your acceptance of the revised Policy.

contact.title: 11. Contact Us
paragraphs: For any questions, requests, or complaints regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact the Data Controller:
